zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Baker於1535年出生在英國瓦維其可爾洛文頓時，她的父親Richard Baker26歲，母親Agnes23歲。她於1558年與Thomas Eaton在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1584年10月22日在她的家鄉享年49歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel Baker于1535年出生在英国瓦維其可爾洛文頓时，她的父亲Richard Baker26岁，母亲Agnes23岁。她于1558年与Thomas Eaton在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1584年10月22日在她的家乡享年49岁。

nameMeanings.name: Baker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name, from Middle English bakere, Old English bæcere, a derivative of bacan ‘to bake’. It may have been used for someone whose special task in the kitchen of a great house or castle was the baking of bread, but since most humbler households did their own baking in the Middle Ages, it may also have referred to the owner of a communal oven used by the whole village. The right to be in charge of this and exact money or loaves in return for its use was in many parts of the country a hereditary feudal privilege. Compare Miller . Less often the surname may have been acquired by someone noted for baking particularly fine bread or by a baker of pottery or bricks.

Americanized form (translation into English) of surnames meaning ‘baker’, for example Dutch Bakker , German Becker and Beck , French Boulanger and Bélanger (see Belanger ), Czech Pekař, Slovak Pekár, and Croatian Pekar .

History: Baker was established as an early immigrant surname in Puritan New England. Among others, two men called Remember Baker (father and son) lived at Woodbury, CT, in the early 17th century, and an Alexander Baker arrived in Boston, MA, in 1635.

variants.name: Miller
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for a miller. The standard modern vocabulary word represents the northern Middle English term miller, an agent derivative of mille ‘mill’, reinforced by Old Norse mylnari (see Milner ). In southern, western, and central England Millward (literally, ‘mill keeper’) was the usual term. In North America, the surname Miller has absorbed many cognate surnames from other languages, for example German Müller (see Mueller ), Dutch Mulder and Molenaar , French Meunier , Italian Molinaro , Spanish Molinero , Hungarian Molnár (see Molnar ), Slovenian, Croatian, and Serbian Mlinar , Polish Młynarz or Młynarczyk (see Mlynarczyk ). Miller (including in the senses below) is the seventh most frequent surname in the US.

South German, Swiss German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of Müller ‘miller’ (see Mueller ) and, in North America, also an altered form of this. This form of the surname is also found in other European countries, notably in Poland, Denmark, France (mainly Alsace and Lorraine), and Czechia; compare 3 below.
Americanized form of Polish, Czech, Croatian, Serbian, and Slovenian   Miler   ‘miller’, a surname of German origin.

variants.name: Baxter
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English baxter ‘baker’ (from Old English bæcestre ‘baker’, earlier ‘female baker’, the feminine equivalent of bæcere). Compare Baker .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Baker was born in 1535, in Rowington, Warwickshire, England, her father, Richard Baker, was 26 and her mother, Agnes, was 23. She married Thomas Eaton in 1558, in Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 22 October 1584, in her hometown, at the age of 49.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel Baker est née en 1535, à Rowington, Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard Baker, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Agnes, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Eaton en 1558, à Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 22 octobre 1584, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 49 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel Baker 1535 in Rowington, Warwickshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard Baker 26 und ihre Mutter Agnes 23. Sie hat 1558 in Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom Thomas Eaton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb am 22. Oktober 1584 im Alter von 49 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Baker è nata il nel 1535 nel Rowington, Warwickshire, England, sua padre, Richard Baker, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Agnes, aveva 23 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Eaton nel 1558, nel Warwickshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 22 ottobre 1584, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 49 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Baker(이)가 1535년 영국 워윅실르 로윙턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Baker(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1558년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르에서 Thomas Eaton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1584년 10월 22일 그녀의 고향에서 49세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Baker nasceu em 1535, em Rowington, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Baker, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Agnes, tinha 23 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Eaton em 1558, em Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 22 de outubro de 1584, em sua cidade natal, com 49 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel Baker родилась в 1535 г. в местности роуингтон, варвиккшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard Baker было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Eaton в 1558 г. в местности варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 22 октября 1584 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 49 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel Baker nació en 1535, en Rowington, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Baker, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Agnes, tenía 23 años.
